purpose: load_graphml's attribute-coercion cluster, characterized at the pin before authoring — every malformed input below is a silent coercion, never a raise — and observed through save_graph_attributes_to_trace=true so the t=0 'graph-attribute-fact' rows reach the change store and trace (interpretation.py:1538): score=0.4 becomes bound [0.4,1] under the bare key (graphml_parser.py:35-39; lower from the value, upper forced to 1) and edge w=0.5 likewise [0.5,1] (graphml_parser.py:65-69); flag=1 becomes [1,1]; flag=0 and pair='0,1' coerce to the vacuous default interval [0,1] and leave NO observable row — the no-change update is suppressed at the gate, so absence from nodes-flag/nodes-pair (only N3 appears under flag) is the banked evidence; pair2='0,0' int-parses in range and sets both bounds, [0.0,0.0] with the label reset to the bare key (graphml_parser.py:44-55); fpair='0.3,0.7' hits int()'s ValueError which is silently swallowed (graphml_parser.py:54-55) and falls through to the composed label 'fpair-0.3,0.7' at [1,1] — the comma-float pair never becomes a bound; big=1.5 (out of range) and word='abc' compose 'big-1.5' / 'word-abc' at [1,1] (graphml_parser.py:40-43) and nodes-bare-keys confirms nothing lands under their bare keys — the bound-attr-numeric-in-range, bound-attr-zero-one-edges, bound-attr-comma-pair, malformed-attr-comma-float, malformed-attr-out-of-range and malformed-attr-nonnumeric classes; the inert rule exists only to clear reason()'s no-rules guard.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md fn:load_graphml bound/malformed attribute classes
